title: Kraken cuts 150 staff amid AI efficiencies, potentially delaying IPO: Report
summary: Kraken has cut 150 staff due to the rising use of AI, adding to more than 5,000 layoffs across the crypto sector so far this year, Bloomberg reports.

title: Verus Ethereum bridge reportedly exploited for $11.6M in latest DeFi attack
summary: Two security companies have flagged the address where the stolen funds are allegedly being held, showing the cryptocurrencies have been converted into 5,402 Ether.

title: US Fuels Hopes for Wider China Crop Trade, Lifting Grain Prices
summary: An agreement on US agricultural sales to China outlined by the White House is raising optimism for a pickup beyond soybeans, further reviving crop flows that have been weighed down by trade tensions.

title: India Can Ride Out Disruption After Russian Oil Waiver Lapse
summary: India’s oil refiners are expected to weather disruption in supply after the US allowed its waiver on Russian crude purchases to expire, thanks to weaker demand and available barrels from the US and the Middle East.

title: Crypto ATM Operator Bitcoin Depot Files for Chapter 11 Bankruptcy
summary: North America's largest Bitcoin ATM operator cited a hostile regulatory environment and an 'unsustainable' business model.

title: KuCoin Australia’s ‘Evolution’ Showcases Regulatory Focus, Mastercard Launch
summary: The exchange’s KuCard rollout enables users to make "seamless payments" to Mastercard merchants using USDC.

title: Bernstein says Clarity Act yield compromise cements Circle’s edge amid record stablecoin supply
summary: Bernstein analysts said the Senate Clarity Act markup and yield compromise strengthen Circle's model as dollar stablecoin supply grows.

title: Iran-linked report says Hormuz Safe bitcoin-settled insurance platform aims to generate $10 billion
summary: Iranian media reported the launch of Hormuz Safe, a bitcoin-settled insurance platform tied to Strait of Hormuz cargo transit.

title: Mortgage costs rise sharply on Middle East conflict
summary: Home loans have become more expensive in North America and Europe despite central banks keeping rates on hold

title: Iran war hits Americans with $40bn fuel bill
summary: Extra spending on petrol and diesel exceeds cost of repairing country’s bridges or rebooting air traffic control
